subject: Physics
title: Halley's Comet Returns
description: A short lesson to help you apply Halley's Comet Returns.

hook.text: In 1705, Edmond Halley predicted a comet would return in 1758. He was right—but died before it came.

buildup.text: Halley studied bright comets from 1531, 1607, and 1682. He noticed a 75-year pattern.

discovery.text: Using Newton's gravity equations, he proved these were the same comet on a repeating orbit.

twist.text: When it returned on schedule, it proved gravity predicts motion of objects far beyond Earth.

climax.text: Halley's Comet last visited in 1986. It will return in 2061, right on Newton's schedule.

punchline.text: A comet that keeps its appointments. Gravity keeps the clock.

quiz.question: How did Halley predict the comet's return?
options: He used Newton's gravity equations to identify a repeating orbit
He found ancient records describing the same comet
He observed the comet's tail changing direction
